eny_0007_mimicw.name: Tunneling Nidwyrm
eny_0007_mimicw.description: A creature that spews highly corrosive fluids. Nidwyrms usually live underground. They would dissolve metallic materials with acids before consuming them. Nidwyrm hunters are therefore advised to take proper care of their weapons.
abilities: Avoid the poisonous spit. Stepping on it will deal continuous Nature DMG to the operator.

eny_0018_lbtough.name: Bonekrusher Executioner
eny_0018_lbtough.description: A Landbreaker armed with a heavy axe. These massive brutes are way more powerful than their common counterparts and can easily damage defensive works or flip over armored vehicles.
abilities: His grab attack deals massive DMG. If an operator is grabbed, use another operator's skill to interrupt him and free the grabbed operator.
Dodging the charged double axe swings will not be easy. Try to interrupt him instead.

eny_0021_agmelee.name: Ram
eny_0021_agmelee.description: The "Kriosson" (archive ID: ACL071) is amongst the earliest recorded form of the Aggeloi and the most commonly encountered. It attacks by ramming its victims with its horns.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0023_aghornb.name: Heavy Ram
eny_0023_aghornb.description: The "Thrylissoron" (archive ID: ACL072) is a non-standard variant of ACL071. It can manipulate its environment and make tactical decisions.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0025_agrange.name: Sting
eny_0025_agrange.description: The "Kentron" (archive ID: ARL121) would charge pieces of itself with energy and propel them with inertial launching. The energy efficiency of this mechanism is extremely low.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0027_agscorp.name: Heavy Sting
eny_0027_agscorp.description: The "Varykentron" (archive ID: ARL141) is a non-standard variant of ARL121. It would occasionally perform whip attacks with its tail instead of using its ranged energy attack.
abilities: This enemy attacks by causing Originium pillars to erupt under the operators.
When approached, this enemy will summon stone pillars around itself for protection.

eny_0029_lbmob.name: Bonekrusher Raider
eny_0029_lbmob.description: A Landbreaker armed with basic melee weapons. These "common rabble" of LB clans tend to be poorly equipped and usually appear in groups.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0033_lbhunt.name: Bonekrusher Ambusher
eny_0033_lbhunt.description: A Landbreaker armed with basic ranged weaponry. Ambushers only received the most rudimentary training and their shots are inaccurate. Nevertheless, their bolts have claimed more than a few lives.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0039_agcanno.name: Sentinel
eny_0039_agcanno.description: The "Phrourossaron" (ARS316) is a suspected Aggeloid attempt at mimicking the shape and attack patterns of humanity's sentry guns. Despite its massive range, the Sentinel is a sessile organism.
abilities: This enemy has a powerful attack with extremely long range. Dodge its bombardment to progress.

eny_0045_agtrinit.name: Triaggelos
eny_0045_agtrinit.description: NO PREVIOUS RECORDS. This has been classified as an Aggelos due to the nature of its formation. Changes to its halo may reflect its ability to make tactical decisions. Be warned. Ankhor-like properties have been detected in this entity as well.
abilities: An enemy with 3 different forms.
ASSAULT FORM: Continuously unleashes melee attacks. Interrupt the charging of its ability to apply high Stagger to it.
SEARCH FORM: Summons lots of Aggeloi to the fight and unleashes Ankhor shockwaves.
TRICKERY FORM: Enters stealth to unleash barrages of projectiles from afar or ambush your operators. Break its stealth mode to apply high Stagger to it.

eny_0046_lbshamman.name: Bonekrusher Pyromancer
eny_0046_lbshamman.description: A Landbreaker who can wield the Originium Arts of Evocation. Pyromancers are extremely dangerous as they can easily ignite the battlefield with their fiery arts.
abilities: This enemy has powerful ranged attacks that deal Heat DMG, but it has poor mobility.
This enemy causes hazefyre to erupt from the ground to deal Heat DMG. Try to dodge the attack.
Instantly teleports somewhere far away when opponents get too close.

eny_0047_firebat.name: Bonekrusher Arsonist
eny_0047_firebat.description: A Landbreaker armed with a flamethrower. Although these weapons look shoddily made, they serve their intended purpose well enough. Arsonists can easily clear out strong points and send the defenders, however stubborn, running for their lives.
abilities: This enemy can continuously spew fire at the operators. Try to move behind this enemy to avoid the flames.

eny_0048_hvybow.name: Bonekrusher Ballista
eny_0048_hvybow.description: A Landbreaker armed with a heavy ranged weapon. Ballistas are elites selected from Ambushers who proved their worth. Their heavy bolts are capable of punching right through heavy body armor.
abilities: Fires a barrage of arrows that deal DMG over time and explode after a delay of several seconds.
Despite being armed with a giant ballista, this enemy is also a skilled melee combatant.

eny_0049_rogue.name: Bonekrusher Infiltrator
eny_0049_rogue.description: A Landbreaker armed with melee weapons. Infiltrators are extremely agile and capable combatants who fight with two weapons at once. They are not to be trifled with.
abilities: A very agile enemy and capable of dodging the operators' basic attacks. However, when she attacks, she won't be able to dodge.
Can throw darts and perform additional attacks on operators hit by the darts. Dodging the darts might offer a good chance to counterattack.

eny_0050_hound.name: Bonekrusher Ripptusk
eny_0050_hound.description: A small warbeast controlled by the Landbreakers. Ripptusks are far more bloodthirsty and ferocious than their untamed wilderness kin. They are absolutely loyal to the Landbreakers who tamed them.
abilities: This enemy is agile but can be overwhelmed when taking multiple hits.

eny_0051_rodin.name: Rhodagn the Bonekrushing Fist
eny_0051_rodin.description: The right fist of Nefarith and the most feared Bonekrusher soldier of the clan. Many Landbreakers had challenged Nefarith's leadership, only to be utterly crushed by Rhodagn's fists.
abilities: A super heavy enemy. He uses ventilation pipes to pull in his opponents so that they are close enough to be attacked.
Buffs himself with the power of denstacks. Watch and evade his jets of fire.

eny_0052_palesent.name: Marble Aggelomoirai
eny_0052_palesent.description: NO PREVIOUS RECORDS. This has been categorized as an Aggelos due to the nature of its formation. Its outward appearance mimics that of Nefarith. Despite its high core energy readings, the entity seems to be rather unstable.
abilities: The awakened Marble Aggelomoirai can summon countless Ankhors from the universe to bombard the land. Dodge these attacks to find a way to retaliate.

eny_0053_hsmob.name: Road Plunderer
eny_0053_hsmob.description: An armed criminal active in the jurisdiction of Wuling City. Road Plunderers would usually set up illegal road barricades or engage in the smuggling of illegal goods. Eliminating these brigands is a top priority for the law enforcement forces of Hongshan.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0054_hsmino.name: Hill Smasher
eny_0054_hsmino.description: A criminal armed with a heavy weapon. The name may sound like an exaggerated joke, but the Hill Smasher's quadruple rocket launcher delivers serious firepower. Do not underestimate this foe.
abilities: Uses the heavy rocket launcher as a melee weapon. Also shoots fireworks for its ranged attacks. Getting rammed and lifted by its horns might set off some super fireworks!
Don't try to backstab it. It has a powerful hind kick!

eny_0055_hscrane.name: Cloud Stalker
eny_0055_hscrane.description: A criminal armed with a strange weapon that appears like a harmless lantern. Do not be fooled. The Cloud Stalker's "lantern" is actually a flying decapitator with terrifying reach.
abilities: Spews disrupting smoke. Operators caught in the smoke suffer shorter line of sight, slower movement speed, and cannot dodge.
The enemy is a relatively poor combatant. Try to take it out first.
Spews green smoke that heals other enemies.

eny_0057_dog.name: Wild Tuskbeast
eny_0057_dog.description: Wild tuskbeasts are predators of the Talosian wilderness. Given the hostile environments of Talos-II, getting to the top of the food chain is no small feat.
abilities: A docile and timid creature. It will not attack operators on sight.

eny_0058_agdisk.name: Effigy
eny_0058_agdisk.description: The "Homoiossaron" (archive ID: AGL371) is capable of mimicking human forms or parts thereof. Some of these rare and dangerous entities are capable of channeling the Æther itself.
abilities: This enemy has powerful slam attacks but rotates slowly during casting. This is the best time to go behind it and attack.
The enemy can reassemble its body in various forms and move quickly across the battlefield.
The enemy will attempt to trap the operators! Quick interruption will counter it.

eny_0059_erhound.name: Hazefyre Tuskbeast
eny_0059_erhound.description: A tuskbeast corrupted by hazefyre. These small aberrations usually move in packs and can pose a serious threat, even to fully armed field teams.
abilities: Less intelligent but more ferocious than common tuskbeasts. Dodge its pounce attack to make it fall to the ground.

eny_0060_lbmad.name: Hazefyre Claw
eny_0060_lbmad.description: A Landbreaker combatant corrupted by hazefyre. Absorbing hazefyre should not be regarded as a fortunate blessing. These raiders have become horribly twisted versions of their former selves.
abilities: Dodge its gnashing attack to make it fall to the ground.

eny_0061_palecore.name: Marble Aggelomoirai
eny_0061_palecore.description: NO PREVIOUS RECORDS. This has been classified as an Aggelos due to the nature of its formation. Though its morphic modules were re-assembled, the entity continues to mimic Nefarith's appearance. No satisfactory explanations have been proposed for this phenomenon.
abilities: Defeat all Marble Appendages to force the dormant Marble Aggelomoirai to temporarily expose its vulnerable core to the attack.

eny_0062_paletent.name: Marble Appendage
eny_0062_paletent.description: NO PREVIOUS RECORDS. This has been classified as an Aggelos due to the nature of its formation. Its physical manifestation would appear like extensions reaching out from its main body. However, these appendages actually reach into the main body and have been postulated to serve as a form of distraction.
abilities: Some Marble Appendages are skilled at attacking melee opponents with powerful slams.
Some Marble Appendages are skilled at attacking ranged opponents by converting their tips into Blight material that can be thrown.

eny_0063_agmelee2.name: Ram α
eny_0063_agmelee2.description: The "Kriosson Anavon" (archive ID: ACL071α) is an energized variant of ACL071. The entity's excessive energy content gives it a characteristic bright red appearance and makes it stronger and faster than the standard variant.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0064_agrange2.name: Sting α
eny_0064_agrange2.description: The "Kentron Anavon" (archive ID: ARL121α) is an energized variant of ARL121. The entity's higher energy density gives it greater firepower. The energy efficiency of its firing mechanism remains low.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0065_lbmob2.name: Elite Raider
eny_0065_lbmob2.description: An elite Landbreaker melee combatant enhanced by fumes known as hazefyre. This particular LB has better gear, strength, and battle skills.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0066_lbhunt2.name: Elite Ambusher
eny_0066_lbhunt2.description: An elite Landbreaker ranged combatant enhanced by fumes known as hazefyre. This particular LB is better trained and more accurate, and is armed with better crossbow and bolts.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0067_hound2.name: Elite Ripptusk
eny_0067_hound2.description: An elite Landbreaker warbeast enhanced by fumes known as hazefyre. This particular ripptusk is more ferocious and cunning, and attacks with less predictable moves.
abilities: Nimble and agile. Will keep trying to approach and attack its target.

eny_0068_lbtough2.name: Elite Executioner
eny_0068_lbtough2.description: An elite Landbreaker brute enhanced by fumes known as hazefyre. This particular LB is a towering giant armed with a great axe. Such elites are usually seen leading Bonekrusher raiding mobs.
abilities: His grab attack deals massive DMG. If an operator is grabbed, use another operator's skill to interrupt him and free the grabbed operator.
Dodging the charged double axe swings will not be easy. Try to interrupt him instead.

eny_0069_aghornb2.name: Heavy Ram α
eny_0069_aghornb2.description: The "Thrylissoron Anavon" (archive ID: ACL072α) is an energized variant of ACL072. Its high energy contents did not affect its decision-making abilities. This entity is extremely dangerous.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0070_agscorp2.name: Heavy Sting α
eny_0070_agscorp2.description: The "Varykentron Anavon" (archive ID: ARL141α) is an energized variant of ARL141 that has retained a plethora of attack patterns. It is a tougher and more dangerous entity.
abilities: This enemy attacks by causing Originium pillars to erupt under the operators.
When approached, this enemy will summon stone pillars around itself for protection.

eny_0071_sandb.name: Axe Armorbeast
eny_0071_sandb.description: A wild axe armorbeast. The creature is clad with bony plates. It can even ram open thick, heavy alloy gates with its axe-shaped horns.
abilities: A raging Axe Armorbeast will use its massive horns to chase out enemies intruding its territories. Watch out for its ramming charge.
The enemy will perform various large area attacks. Interrupt it when it becomes Enraged.

eny_0072_slimeml.name: Indigenous Pincerbeast
eny_0072_slimeml.description: A wild creature infected by Originium. The large beast carries multiple Originium clusters and is capable of ripping apart its prey with its sickle-like frontal limbs.
abilities: An enemy that shows up in groups. Watch out for their razor sharp forward appendages.

eny_0073_slimerg.name: Acid Originium Slug
eny_0073_slimerg.description: A wild creature infected by Originium. This slug secretes an acidic material. When threatened, it will attempt to intimidate its foe by shaking violently and projecting shards from the Originium cluster carried on its back.
abilities: A timid enemy that retreats into its shell to protect itself. It will also mindlessly fire Originium crystals from its body to intimidate its opponents.

eny_0074_lbshield.name: Bonekrusher Vanguard
eny_0074_lbshield.description: A Bonekrusher combatant armed with spears and shields. Though armed with weapons crudely cobbled together, the vanguard has developed a combat technique that unites attack and defense. Practice caution when fighting this foe.
abilities: This enemy will block all incoming attacks unless he is attacking. Stagger him to shatter his shield.

eny_0075_lbroshan.name: Bonekrusher Siegeknuckles
eny_0075_lbroshan.description: A Landbreaker combatant equipped with heavy explosive gauntlets. These serve as the mainstay of Bonekrusher mobs. A Siegeknuckles's gauntlets serve as portable weapons for taking down fortified positions.
abilities: A relentless enemy. Even after being interrupted, he is likely going to try attacking again.
This enemy uses his armored sleeve to scatter reactive armor scales. Keep a safe distance, especially when accidentally stepping on it.
This enemy will try to retrieve armor scales from the ground to get back to form, and will launch a powerful attack upon successful retrieval.

eny_0076_agfly.name: Falsewings
eny_0076_agfly.description: The "Psuthopteron" (archive ID: ARA07C) has flight capabilities that are wholly unrelated to its morphology. This pattern was originally regarded as biomimicry. However, the modern consensus believes it was a mimic of Terran flying vehicles.
abilities: Launches small whirlwinds that can also slow down operators.

eny_0077_agshield.name: Walking Chrysopolis
eny_0077_agshield.description: The "Thureoron" (archive ID: ACL156) is a pattern furnished with a shield-like structure that protects its halo. Be warned. This Aggelos can manipulate energy to drag distant targets towards itself.
abilities: The Walking Chrysopolis has two states: Normal and Bastion.
Normal State: This has the ability to pull in operators from afar.
Bastion State: All incoming attacks on its front or sides will be reflected, but this also exposes a weak point on its back.

eny_0078_nefarp1.name: Nefarith, "Bonekrusher"
eny_0078_nefarp1.description: Thanks to Nefarith, the "Bonekrushers" became a byword for terror in the Civilization Band. Her brutal conquest has not yet ended, and her true ambitions have not yet been revealed.
abilities: Unleashes Æther energy waves that cannot be dodged. Use Zhuang Fangyi's Xiranite to leap into mid-air and avoid it completely.
Leaps to great heights to charge-up for an attack. To interrupt this enemy move, use Xiranite to jump into the air, then perform a dive attack to summon a lightning strike.

eny_0079_nefarp2.name: Nefarith, "Conqueror"
eny_0079_nefarp2.description: The Conqueror's inner perception of herself. She does not hide her desire to lay waste to Civilization.
abilities: Constantly protected by an Æther shield that greatly reduces the DMG taken by the enemy. Interrupt the enemy's charge-up to temporarily disable the shield.
Tears the fabric of spacetime to immobilize your operators. Try to interrupt this skill if you can.
Traps your operators with an Ankhor. Attack the Ankhor to free them.

eny_0080_reaper.name: Ereignis
eny_0080_reaper.description: What is the meaning of progress? What constitutes meaning? What is the nature of life?

eny_0081_ruanyi.name: Ruan Yi
eny_0081_ruanyi.description: Ruan Yi had kept his anger in check for years. When a fleeting "opportunity" presented itself, he decided it was time to finally unleash his pent-up fury.
abilities: Ruan Yi can deflect all attacks. Use Xiranite to break his protection to deal effective DMG to him.
At low HP, this enemy enters an Infuriated state. Use Xiranite to break this state and apply Weakened on the enemy for an extended period.
When Infuriated, Ruan Yi can summon Wrothful Roots of Bane that explode when the controlled operator gets too close.
Xiranite Energy will coalesce and appear in the battlefield to offer support. Touch the Xiranite Energy at the right timing to activate it and suppress Ruan Yi.

eny_0082_hsbear.name: Skydrummer
eny_0082_hsbear.description: A howlerbeast that grew up in the wilderness. This beast is indigenous to Talos-II. Strong membranes on its head and neck allow it to unleash powerful howling loud enough to shake the hills and forests.
abilities: Low HP triggers the enemy's Enraged state. During this state, the enemy performs its charge-up attacks more often, deals more DMG, and gains DMG Reduction. Interrupt the enemy in time to end the Enraged state.

eny_0083_hstiger.name: Spotted Rakerbeast
eny_0083_hstiger.description: A rakerbeast that grew up in the wilderness. These apex predators are endemic to Talos-II. They can leap great distances and attack their prey with razor sharp claws.
abilities: An agile beast that attacks with its claws and tail. Try to dodge.
Enraged State: When Enraged, the beast has increased ATK and uses its charge up skills more often. Applying Staggered to it ends the Enraged state.

eny_0084_hshunt.name: Grove Archer
eny_0084_hshunt.description: An armed outlaw equipped with basic ranged weaponry. Trained in the bamboo groves, the outlaw can fire an arrow through the forest without hitting a single leaf blade.
abilities: Fires all sorts of arrows and throws bombs to harass their opponents from range.

eny_0085_hsrogue.name: Nimbus Razor
eny_0085_hsrogue.description: A criminal armed with a strange weapon. When the Cloud Stalker is tired of using the strange lantern, he would draw his short blade and engage in melee combat.
abilities: Uses chain hooks to attack allied operators. Chained operators will be Stunned unless freed.

eny_0087_wgslime.name: Mudflow
eny_0087_wgslime.description: The "Tholeroygran" (archive ID: ACM311) is the first fluid-based pattern to be discovered. The morphology of the halo is unstable and the internal temperature is typically lower than its constituent materials.
abilities: This enemy can quickly close the distance from afar. Its attacks can apply Cryo Infliction to operators upon hit.

eny_0088_wgthorns.name: Hedron
eny_0088_wgthorns.description: The "Diaphaistagran" (archive ID: ARM315) is a fluid-based pattern with energy projecting capabilities. Its shape is that of a complex yet symmetrical geometry. Its attacks can also sap heat from any surface they come in contact with.
abilities: The projectiles it launches apply Cryo Infliction to operators upon hit.

eny_0089_wgreflec.name: Prism
eny_0089_wgreflec.description: The "Prismastagran" (archive ID: ASM316) is a non-model variant of ARM315. It is capable of protecting nearby Aggeloi, and is very difficult to hit as it often surrounds itself with ARM315 entities.
abilities: This enemy can greatly strengthen nearby allies to improve their capabilities.

eny_0090_wgabyss.name: Tidalklast
eny_0090_wgabyss.description: The "Anavatklydan" (archive ID: AGM32L) is a non-model variant of AGM321. Such entities come in a wide range of sizes. The sight of particularly large individuals breaking out of the water has become a nightmare for those who witnessed it.
abilities: The enemy has two states: Ranged and Melee.
Melee State: In this state, the enemy will try to approach the target to unleash melee attacks.
Ranged State: In this state, the enemy will maintain its distance and attack with more powerful abilities.
The ranged state suffers more Heat DMG, but has significant DMG Reduction against other elements.

eny_0091_wgshoal.name: Tidewalker
eny_0091_wgshoal.description: The "Genesklydan" (archive ID: AGM321) exhibits humanoid head and legs. Ever since the discovery of this particular pattern, human resemblance became an unspoken rule for assessing Aggeloid threat levels.
abilities: Attacks with abilities that apply Cryo Infliction, and applies Solidification to operators it hits.

eny_0092_slbomb.name: Firemist Originium Slug
eny_0092_slbomb.description: A wild creature infected by Originium with extremely high body temperatures. This slug explodes when near death, covering everything nearby with acidic body fluids at boiling temperatures as well as active Originium dust.
abilities: This enemy self-detonates at low HP. The detonation applies an unstable material on the operator it hits. If the operator takes DMG, the material will explode.

eny_0093_hshog.name: Quillbeast
eny_0093_hshog.description: A wild quillbeast indigenous to Talos-II. When it raises its quills and starts rolling around, it becomes as destructive as a spinning chainsaw.
abilities: Enraged State: When Enraged, the beast has increased ATK and uses its charge up skills more often. Applying Staggered to it ends the Enraged state.

eny_0094_hsfly.name: Waterlamp
eny_0094_hsfly.description: A wild arthropod that lives in aquatic environments. The waterlamp gives off a faint glow and its body fluids contain irritants that may induce allergic reactions and even burns. Be careful when tackling this creature.
abilities: An enemy that spews acids stored in its head. Operators hit by this attack will suffer reduced DEF. Though not very threatening, care should be taken in any case.

eny_0095_ethillu.name: Æthillu
eny_0095_ethillu.description: A phantom that answered Ardashir's call. It is a manifestation of destruction, a sedimentation of ancient senescence.
abilities: This enemy has a powerful revival ability. The revived form has a longer attack range. It can also detonate itself to deal massive DMG.

eny_0096_hsmob2.name: Highway Reaver
eny_0096_hsmob2.description: An armed criminal active in Wuling territories. They would usually set up illegal road barricades or engage in the smuggling of illegal goods. Some of them would even attack travelers.
abilities: This enemy has no special abilities.

eny_0097_hsmino2.name: Cloud Obliterator
eny_0097_hsmino2.description: A criminal equipped with heavy weaponry. Obliteration is no small feat. Only the strongest smashers armed with quadruple rocket launchers can achieve the title of Cloud Obliterator.
abilities: Uses the heavy rocket launcher as a melee weapon. Also shoots fireworks for its ranged attacks. Getting rammed and lifted by its horns might set off some super fireworks!
Don't try to backstab it. It has a powerful hind kick!

eny_0098_sandb2.name: Hazefyre Axe Armorbeast
eny_0098_sandb2.description: An axe armorbeast corrupted by hazefyre. The creature has become extremely aggressive, having been driven into a state of rabid insanity. Approach with caution if you do not intend to be split in half by its "great axe".
abilities: A raging Axe Armorbeast will use its massive horns to chase out enemies intruding its territories. Watch out for its ramming charge.
The enemy will perform various large area attacks. Interrupt it when it becomes Enraged.

eny_0099_slimeml2.name: Brutal Pincerbeast
eny_0099_slimeml2.description: A wild creature infected by Originium. The giant beast carries numerous clusters of Originium that exhibit higher levels of activity. It also sports a thicker carapace than its ordinary counterparts.
abilities: An enemy that shows up in groups. Watch out for their razor sharp forward appendages.

eny_0100_slimerg2.name: Acid Originium Slug α
eny_0100_slimerg2.description: A wild creature infected by Originium. This acid Originium slug is more hazardous than its ordinary part. The Originium cluster on its back is gaining higher levels of activity and makes the creature increasingly aggressive.
abilities: A timid enemy that retreats into its shell to protect itself. It will also mindlessly fire Originium crystals from its body to intimidate its opponents.

eny_0101_agfly2.name: Falsewings α
eny_0101_agfly2.description: The "Psuthopteron Anavon" (archive ID: ARA07Cα) is an energized variant of the ARA07C. Most observers regard color as the only differentiating feature between the Anavon and the basic variants.
abilities: Launches small whirlwinds that can also slow down operators.

eny_0102_hstiger2.name: Glaring Rakerbeast
eny_0102_hstiger2.description: A rakerbeast variant featuring a different colored coat, shorter lifespan, but greater strength. Its eyes seem to glow like liquid fire.
abilities: When compared to common rakerbeasts, this enemy will enter temporary stealth and charge up its skills at low HP. Interrupt it quickly to end its stealth and deal massive Stagger to it.
Enraged State: When Enraged, the beast has increased ATK and uses its charge up skills more often. Applying Staggered to it ends the Enraged state.

eny_0103_hshunt2.name: Sweeping Wind
eny_0103_hshunt2.description: A criminal armed with basic ranged weaponry. Keen sight and dexterous hands allow for deadly shots. This Wind would blow through buildings to sweep all the valuables away.
abilities: Fires all sorts of arrows and throws bombs to harass their opponents from range.

eny_0104_hsrogue2.name: Breaking Gust
eny_0104_hsrogue2.description: A criminal armed with an exotic weapon. Such Cloud Stalkers are so skilled in hooked blades people claim they could slice and dice the very clouds into perfect cubes.
abilities: Uses chain hooks to attack allied operators. Chained operators will be Stunned unless freed.

eny_0105_wgslime2.name: Mudflow δ
eny_0105_wgslime2.description: The "Tholeroygran " (archive ID: ACM311δ) is an energized variant of ACM311. It utilizes unique energy pathways and thus maintains a homeostatic temperature significantly below the freezing points of its constituent matter.
abilities: This enemy can quickly close the distance from afar. Its attacks can apply Cryo Infliction to operators upon hit.

eny_0106_wgthorns2.name: Hedron δ
eny_0106_wgthorns2.description: The "Diaphaistagran Dinedon" (archive ID: ARM315δ) is an energized variant of ARM315. When irradiated by sunlight, it gives off a shimmering purple light. However, it assumes a different form when irradiated by light reflecting off Talos.
abilities: The projectiles it launches apply Cryo Infliction to operators upon hit.

eny_0107_wgshoal2.name: Tidewalker δ
eny_0107_wgshoal2.description: The "Genesklydan Dinedon" (archive ID: AGM321δ) is an energized variant of AGM321. Its constituent matter is in a constant, turbulent flow. This also explains its particularly terrifying form when it appears at night.
abilities: Attacks with abilities that apply Cryo Infliction, and applies Solidification to operators it hits.

eny_0108_slbomb2.name: Blazemist Originium Slug
eny_0108_slbomb2.description: A wild creature infected by Originium with extremely high body temperatures. Its dorsal carapace is imbued with Originium dust with higher levels of activity.
abilities: This enemy self-detonates at low HP. The detonation applies an unstable material on the operator it hits. If the operator takes DMG, the material will explode.

eny_0109_hshog2.name: Imbued Quillbeast
eny_0109_hshog2.description: A quillbeast infected by active Originium dust. The creature's spines are imbued with high levels of energy, making them extremely destructive.
abilities: This enemy will charge up and inflate its abdomen to spew superhot lava from its body. Interrupt it during charge up to deal massive Stagger to it.
Enraged State: When Enraged, the beast has increased ATK and uses its charge up skills more often. Applying Staggered to it ends the Enraged state.

eny_0112_jzmonk.name: Rockhowler
eny_0112_jzmonk.description: Rockhowlers can live in the natural wilderness. These intelligent yet mischievous creatures often prepare "traps" and "dangers" for hapless travelers. They seem to enjoy the sight of humans frustrated by their antics. Please deal with them patiently.
abilities: This enemy typically roams around in packs. Any operator or Rockhowler that steps on discarded banana peels will slip and fall.

eny_0113_jzogre.name: Armored Manglerbeast
eny_0113_jzogre.description: A variant of manglerbeasts that evolved specifically on Talos-II. These unique manglers are armored in extremely thick scales and share a mutualistic symbiosis with rockhowlers. The former species provides the bulwark and muscle while the latter offers keen senses and warns against trespassers.
abilities: The Rockhowler riding on the Armored Manglerbeast's back can throw banana peels. If the Armored Manglerbeast steps on banana peels while performing a charging attack, it will slip and fall.
When curled into its defensive pose, only its hands remain exposed as weak spots. Break the hands to end the defensive pose and apply knock down to the beast.
Drops Rockhowlers when knocked down. Attack the Rockhowlers to deal even more DMG.

eny_0114_jzmking.name: Craghowler
eny_0114_jzmking.description: Craghowlers are the dominant members of rockhowler communities. These beasts will fight each other countless times before the strongest champion emerges as the leading craghowler. There is no question that such individuals have mastered combat in ways that their kin could only dream of.
abilities: When enraged, the Craghowler will start breaking the ruins. Loosened rocks will fall into the battlefield and deal DMG to operators hit by them.
The Craghowler can use the pillars within the ruins to great effect and give itself a huge attack range.
The Craghowler will feign a retreat, and suddenly throw huge rocks to attack unsuspecting enemies.
